title: Is the zero-divisor graph Γ(Z_23 × Z_25) prime?
generalization_signal: At p = 23 the four-class witness no longer needs the earlier half-interval spill language: C can be exactly the 22 primes greater than 29 in {1,...,134}, while A absorbs all nontrivial multiples of 19, 23, and 29. This suggests a cleaner barrier-threshold slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25).
strongest_honest_claim: An explicit prime-labeling witness for Γ(Z_23 × Z_25) survives skeptical verification. The campaign-relevant gain is that this feeder closes with the cleaner barrier-threshold package B = {1,19,23,29}, A absorbing all nontrivial barrier multiples, and C taken exactly as the 22 primes greater than 29 in {1,...,134}. That is still feeder evidence rather than a proved family theorem, but it points to a real Γ(Z_p × Z_25) slice.
candidate_theorem_slice: A barrier-threshold sufficient-condition slice for Γ(Z_p × Z_25): if {1,...,5p+19} contains p - 1 usable primes greater than 29 for C and the nontrivial multiples of the fixed pairwise-coprime barrier set B = {1,19,23,29} fit inside the 20 A-slots, then taking A as the barrier-multiple reservoir, C from those primes, and D as the complement yields a prime labeling.
next_action: promote the verified p = 23 barrier-threshold witness into the family dossier, then run generalize before testing z29-z25-prime-zero-divisor-graph; do not escalate this instance alone to Lean yet
